big_picture: Iran has pledged to ensure safe passage for India-bound vessels, including 22 ships stranded near the Strait of Hormuz amid tensions, with efforts underway to address the situation. The ongoing conflict in West Asia has disrupted supplies, leading to price hikes and emergency measures that may burden Indian oil marketing companies. Economists suggest that India's policy interest rate cuts are unlikely to continue due to inflation risks from the West Asia crisis and sustained foreign outflows. Meanwhile, the Union Government revoked the detention of activist Sonam Wangchuk under the National Security Act after five months, following a prolonged legal process. The Supreme Court had questioned the government's actions during hearings on his detention.

articles.title: How persistently high oil prices amid Iran war could impact India's economy
articles.summary: Escalating oil prices due to the Iran war pose a significant threat to India's economy. Government finances may also face pressure from increased subsidies, potentially impacting infrastructure spending and economic growth.

Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman assured the House that this extra expenditure will not impact the fiscal deficit, which remains within the Revised Estimates. The government also reduced its total expenditure projection for the year.

The government also reduced its total expenditure projection for the year.

articles.title: Y Combinator brings Startup School to India; here is what it means for your startup
articles.summary: Launched in 2017 by Y Combinator, one of the world’s most influential startup accelerators and early-stage VC firms, Startup School is a free online course that offers personalised coaching and lectures for aspiring founders. The India leg of the school will target engineers, builders, and entrepreneurs interested in the startup ecosystem.

articles.title: Bombay High Court quashes GST cash seizure, ruling may reshape how tax raids treat currency
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that GST officers cannot seize currency during searches, ordering the return of ₹1 crore to Smruti Waghdhare with interest.

articles.title: RBI revises bank dividend framework, new norms to take effect from FY27
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has repealed its 2025 dividend framework for commercial banks and introduced revised prudential norms, effective FY27, aimed at aligning profit payouts with banks’ capital strength, profitability and asset-quality indicators.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India has repealed its 2025 dividend framework for commercial banks and introduced revised prudential norms, effective FY27, aimed at aligning profit payouts with banks’ capital strength, profitability and asset-quality indicators.

articles.title: Why Indian restaurants are facing LPG shortages: Inside the govt’s gas reprioritisation order
articles.summary: India has invoked Essential Commodities Act, 1955, reprioritising natural gas supply after disruptions to LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz linked to US-Israel-Iran war. The directive redirects gas toward priority sectors such as households, transport and fertiliser production while curtailing supply to some industrial users.

articles.title: Why India’s AI strategy is betting on smaller models
articles.summary: India's AI strategy focuses on small, specialized AI systems and large models. The white paper "Advancing Indigenous Foundation Models" highlights Small Language Models for agriculture and healthcare. Initiatives like Sarvam AI and BharatGen develop models for Indic languages. The IndiaAI Mission supports this with shared infrastructure and datasets for all 22 scheduled Indian languages.

articles.title: How graph databases are poised to become critical for BFSI dependencies
articles.summary: The future of Indian financial services will be shaped by institutions that can see the full picture — the hidden links between customers, transactions, risks and systems. Graph databases, therefore, are not merely a technological upgrade but a fundamental shift in how BFSI organisations understand and act on their data, writes Neo4j's APAC Head Ish Thukral.

articles.title: Supreme Court Seeks Report From Union & States On Removal Of Encroachments From Ganga Banks
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Union and State governments to provide a detailed report on illegal constructions along the Ganga's banks and floodplains. The court's directive follows a case involving encroachments in Patna, Bihar, and highlights the need for action to protect the river. This move underscores the judicial push for environmental conservation and regulatory compliance.

A Bench of Justice JB Pardiwala and Justice KV Viswanathan directed the Union Government, the State of Bihar and other States through which the Ganga flows to place before it updated details on encroachments and the steps taken to protect the riverbanks. articles.title: Abki baar, ‘sources’ sarkar

articles.title: Can women enter Sabarimala? Kerala says neither yes nor no, tells SC to ask scholars, weigh equality

This follows review petitions challenging the court's earlier judgment on the issue, which raises questions about gender equality and religious rights.

articles.summary: PM Modi unveiled projects worth over ₹4,570 crore in Assam's Kokrajhar via video conference. He flagged off three trains and laid the foundation for a POH Workshop at Bashbari.
articles.raw_summary: PM Modi unveiled projects worth over ₹4,570 crore in Assam's Kokrajhar via video conference. He flagged off three trains and laid the foundation for a POH Workshop at Bashbari.

articles.title: FDI policy eased: Relaxed PN3 opens door for global funds with Chinese stakes
articles.summary: In a strategic shift, India has relaxed its foreign investment regulations, facilitating easier entry for companies from the US, Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe that have minimal Chinese ownership.
articles.raw_summary: In a strategic shift, India has relaxed its foreign investment regulations, facilitating easier entry for companies from the US, Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe that have minimal Chinese ownership.

articles.title: No fuel shortage amid war; India stands by US trade deal talks, says Goyal
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal said India sees no fuel shortage despite the war situation and urged citizens not to panic, while reaffirming New Delhi’s commitment to US trade deal talks and outlining steps to support exporters.
articles.raw_summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal said India sees no fuel shortage despite the war situation and urged citizens not to panic, while reaffirming New Delhi’s commitment to US trade deal talks and outlining steps to support exporters.

articles.title: Luxury farmhouse communities emerge as India’s new second-home bet
articles.summary: Rising HNI population, demand for land-led living and new expressway corridors are pushing developers to formalise India’s once-unstructured farmhouse market.
articles.raw_summary: Rising HNI population, demand for land-led living and new expressway corridors are pushing developers to formalise India’s once-unstructured farmhouse market.

articles.title: Churning within TVK; to be lone ''guiding light'' of Tamil Nadu or be part of NDA
articles.summary: TVK party leaders are reportedly discussing a potential alliance with the NDA, despite public denials. Internal discussions aim to leverage the charisma of actor Vijay and the strength of seasoned parties to create electoral synergy. The outcome could impact Tamil Nadu's political landscape ahead of elections.
articles.raw_summary: While TVK senior leader C T R Nirmal Kumar on Saturday denied scope for an alliance with the NDA, multiple party sources said that internal discussions, led by party General Secretary N Anand were ongoing to explore bringing together "Vijay's charisma and the strength of seasoned parties," to provide electoral synergy and ensure a magical victory.

articles.title: West Asia war threatens diamond trade recovery; Crisil sees 10% FY26 revenue dip; better news in FY27
articles.summary: India's diamond industry faces disruption due to the West Asia conflict, threatening key supply routes. GJEPC warns of logistical challenges and rising costs.
articles.raw_summary: India's diamond industry faces disruption due to the West Asia conflict, threatening key supply routes. GJEPC warns of logistical challenges and rising costs.

articles.title: Amid war-driven supply risks, Vedanta’s Agarwal calls for policy stability, simpler rules to boost domestic resources
articles.summary: Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal urges for policy stability, simplified regulations, and entrepreneurial freedom to boost India's domestic production and reduce import dependence.
articles.raw_summary: Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal urges for policy stability, simplified regulations, and entrepreneurial freedom to boost India's domestic production and reduce import dependence.

articles.summary: The Commerce Ministry organized a brainstorming session to strengthen India's medical devices export ecosystem. The initiative aims to enhance the country's position in the global medical devices market. This aligns with broader government efforts to boost exports and improve healthcare infrastructure.

articles.title: केंद्रीय कोयला एवं खान मंत्री जी किशन रेड्डी ने महाराष्ट्र में दो कोयला गैसीकरण परियोजनाओं का भूमि पूजन किया
articles.summary: Union Coal and Mines Minister G. Kishan Reddy performed the groundbreaking ceremony for two coal gasification projects in Maharashtra. These projects are part of India's efforts to enhance energy infrastructure and reduce reliance on imported fuels. The initiative aligns with government policies promoting self-sufficiency in energy resources.

articles.title: पश्चिम एशिया में हाल के घटनाक्रमों पर अंतर-मंत्रालय संवाददाता सम्मेलन
articles.summary: An inter-ministerial press conference was held to discuss recent developments in West Asia. The event involved multiple government ministries and may have implications for India's foreign policy and governance. The details of the discussion and its potential impact on Indian policy are not specified in the available information.

articles.title: सरकार ने कैप्टिव पावर फ्रेमवर्क को मजबूत करने और औद्योगिक प्रतिस्पर्धा को बढ़ावा देने के लिए विद्युत नियमों में संशोधन किया
articles.summary: The Indian government has amended electricity regulations to strengthen the captive power framework and promote industrial competition. This move aims to enhance energy efficiency and support industrial growth. The changes were announced by the government to address regulatory gaps and foster a competitive industrial environment.

articles.title: डॉ. जितेंद्र सिंह ने जम्मू कश्मीर के कठुआ में एक बहुराष्ट्रीय कंपनी की 600 करोड़ रुपए की फार्मा उत्पादन इकाई का शिलान्यास किया
articles.summary: Dr. Jitendra Singh, Union Minister of State for Science and Technology, laid the foundation stone for a 600-crore pharmaceutical production unit in Kathua, Jammu & Kashmir. The project, involving a multinational company, aims to boost local manufacturing and healthcare infrastructure in the region. This initiative aligns with government efforts to promote industrial growth and self-reliance in the pharmaceutical sector.

articles.title: पश्चिम बंगाल के कोलकाता में विभिन्न विकास कार्यों के शुभारंभ के दौरान प्रधानमंत्री के संबोधन का मूल पाठ
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated various development projects in Kolkata, West Bengal. The event highlights the government's focus on infrastructure and regional development. The speech likely emphasized policy initiatives, though the exact details are not provided in the summary.

articles.title: चेन्नई, तमिलनाडु में दिशा योजना के तहत टेली-लॉ कार्यक्रम पर क्षेत्रीय कार्यशाला का आयोजन
articles.summary: A regional workshop was organized in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, under the Disha scheme's Tele-Law program. The event focused on promoting legal awareness and access to justice, aligning with the government's efforts to enhance governance and education in the region.

articles.title: खादी और ग्राम उद्योग आयोग (केवीआईसी) खादी और ग्राम उद्योग क्षेत्र के विकास के लिए ग्रामोद्योग विकास योजना (जीवीवाई) का कार्यान्वयन करता है
articles.summary: The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) is implementing the Gramodyog Vikas Yojana (GVI) to promote the development of the Khadi and village industries sector. This scheme aims to support rural entrepreneurship and economic growth in India's rural areas. The initiative is part of the government's broader efforts to strengthen traditional industries and create employment opportunities.

articles.title: एमएसएमई मंत्रालय ने 364 एमएसई-सीडीपी परियोजनाएँ पूरी कीं; स्‍फूर्ति का पारंपरिक उद्योग समूहों को बढ़ावा
articles.summary: The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) has completed 364 projects under the MSME-Cluster Development Programme (CDP), aimed at promoting traditional industrial clusters. This initiative is part of the government's efforts to support small businesses and boost economic growth in specific sectors.

articles.title: पीएम मुद्रा योजना उद्यमियों और उद्यमों को 20 लाख रुपये तक का बिना गारंटी वाला ऋण प्रदान करती है
articles.summary: The PM MUDRA Yojana offers collateral-free loans of up to ₹20 lakh to entrepreneurs and businesses. This scheme, announced by the government, aims to support small businesses and self-employed individuals by providing easy access to credit without requiring collateral. The initiative is part of broader efforts to boost entrepreneurship and economic growth in India.

articles.title: प्रधानमंत्री श्री नरेन्‍द्र मोदी ने असम के सिलचर में विभिन्न विकास परियोजनाओं के उद्घाटन और शिलान्यास समारोह में अपने भाषण की कुछ झलकियाँ साझा की
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated and laid the foundation for various development projects in Silchar, Assam. The event highlights the government's focus on infrastructure and regional development, which are key policy priorities.

articles.title: केंद्रीय स्वास्थ्य मंत्री श्री जेपी नड्डा ने तीर्थंकर महावीर विश्वविद्यालय के दीक्षांत समारोह को संबोधित किया; स्नातकों से भारत के अमृतकाल के दौरान समाज की सेवा के लिए प्रतिबद्ध रहने का आह्वान किया
articles.summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda addressed the convocation ceremony at Tirthankar Mahaveer University, urging graduates to commit to serving society during India's Amrit Kaal. articles.title: LTCG Cannot Be Taxed in Wife’s Hands When Property Settled by Husband – ITAT Deletes Rs. 19.86 Cr Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that long-term capital gains (LTCG) from property transferred to a spouse without consideration must be taxed in the hands of the transferor (husband) under Section 64(1)(iv). This ruling clarifies tax laws related to inter-spousal property transfers and has implications for tax planning and compliance.

articles.title: APTEL Upholds Tata Power’s Phased Distribution Rollout in Mumbai
articles.summary: The Appellate Tribunal for Electricity (APTEL) has dismissed Brihanmumbai Electric Supply and Transport Undertaking’s (BEST) appeal challenging the Maharashtra Electricity Regulatory Commission’s (MERC) order permitting Tata Power Company (TPCL) to implement a phased rollout of its distribution network in Mumbai. The Tribunal upheld MERC’s June 12, 2017, order, which allowed TPCL to expand its distribution infrastructure in overlapping areas with BEST over a seven-year period in two phases.

The Tribunal upheld MERC’s June 12, 2017, order, which allowed TPCL to expand its distribution infrastructure in overlapping areas with BEST over a seven-year period in two phases.

articles.title: The Hormuz Minefield
articles.summary: The article explains that Iran has a strategic advantage in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route for oil. For India, this situation is relevant because it relies heavily on oil imports through this strait, making it a potential policy concern.

articles.summary: The Middle East conflict has disrupted global oil supplies, forcing Asian countries to implement measures like fuel caps and shorter work weeks. The U.S. has hinted at easing some sanctions, but the crisis remains unresolved.

articles.title: Global food supplies could be badly hit if Iran war drags on, says fertiliser boss
articles.summary: The CEO of Yara International, a major fertiliser company, warns that prolonged conflict in Iran could severely disrupt global food supplies. He highlights the risk of the Strait of Hormuz being closed, which would be 'catastrophic' and drive up food prices in poorer countries. The article calls on global leaders to address the issue before it worsens.

Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Norway’s Yara International, has called on global leaders to consider the impact that soaring food prices will have in some of the world’s poorest countries “before it is too late”

India, as a major importer of oil, may need to consider policy adjustments if the situation escalates.

articles.title: Meta ends end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs: what this means
articles.summary: Meta has ended optional end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs, reverting chats to unencrypted mode after May 8. This gives Meta access to user content for scanning and moderation, prioritizing safety over privacy. The move follows lawsuits over platform harms and may impact discussions on digital rights and surveillance in India.
articles.raw_summary: Instagram had introduced the optional end-to-end encryption in December 2023. Users had to enable it manually per chat, unlike the default encryption available on WhatsApp or Messenger. After May 8, these chats will revert to the unencrypted mode, giving Meta access to content for scanning and moderation. The move follows Instagram prioritising safety over secrecy, amid lawsuits over platform harms.

He has also worked at Thinking Machines Lab, which works on large-scale training infrastructure and tools for foundation models.

articles.summary: The Gulf war is beginning to disrupt key maritime routes used for India’s exports, putting up to $4 billion of monthly shipments at risk if the situation continues for a month, according to the Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO). Exporters are trying to reroute cargo and explore alternative markets as shipping and air logistics face delays and rising costs.

Thailand's currency depreciation is affecting its rice prices.

articles.title: Retail inflation climbs to 3.21% in February
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India rose to 3.21% in February, with food and beverage prices contributing significantly to the increase. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA, noted that the rise was largely driven by the food and beverages segment. This data could influence future policy decisions by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the government.
articles.raw_summary: Sequentially, retail inflation increased 0.1%, while food inflation fell 0.2%. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. "The uptick was almost entirely led by the food and beverages (F B) segment, which accounted for as much as 44 basis points of the 47 bps rise in the headline print between these months," said Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA. One basis point is 0.01 percentage point.

articles.title: Food prices push India's inflation to 3.21% in February

This uptick, the second under the revised CPI series, saw food inflation rise significantly, with personal care and precious metals also contributing to the increase. Future inflation may be impacted by Middle East tensions and rupee depreciation.

articles.title: India's sunflower oil imports halve in February amid West Asia conflict, rising prices
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) said average import price for crude sunflower oil rose 17 per cent to USD 1,420 per tonne in February from USD 1,216 a year earlier, while the rupee's 4.2 per cent depreciation over the past year compounded costs for importers and refiners.

articles.title: India begins probe into circumvention of countervailing duty on saccharin imports
articles.summary: India has launched an investigation into claims that saccharin imports from China are being rerouted through Thailand to avoid existing duties. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies is examining if Chinese-origin saccharin is being falsely declared as originating from Thailand. If confirmed, India may extend countervailing duties to imports from Thailand. This probe impacts industries like food, beverage, and pharmaceuticals.

While food and fuel inflation figures are pending, economists monitor potential imported inflation risks.

articles.title: US begins new unfair trade probe against overproduction in India, 15 others
articles.summary: The US has launched a trade investigation into excess industrial capacity in 16 countries, including India, under Section 301 of its Trade Act. Evidence suggests significant excess capacity in India's petrochemicals, steel, and solar module sectors.

articles.title: India to release product list for faster PN3 FDI approvals
articles.summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.
articles.raw_summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.

articles.title: West Asia conflict could hurt agri input availability: UPL Executive
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are increasing costs for agricultural inputs like fertilizers, with prices rising 50-80%. While current inventories ensure stable supply for the upcoming kharif season, sustained disruptions could force policy interventions to stabilize prices and ensure food security in India.
articles.raw_summary: Geopolitical tensions and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are driving up agricultural input costs, with fertilizer prices already surging 50-80%. While shipping, insurance, and energy costs have climbed, immediate agrochemical availability for the upcoming kharif season remains stable due to pre-built inventories.

articles.title: Iran war impact: Govt mulling diversion of stranded cargo to new markets
articles.summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.
articles.raw_summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.

articles.title: Centre holds back on fuel exports ban due to logistics, tax hurdles as West Asia conflict rages
articles.summary: India is not banning fuel exports despite global supply issues. Logistical hurdles, contractual obligations with overseas buyers, and tax waivers for export-only refineries are key reasons. Reliance Industries and Nayara Energy are major exporters. China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

articles.title: Private sector optimism rises to decade high on strong demand
articles.summary: With the dawn of a new year on the horizon, Indian firms are brimming with optimism. Consumer demand remains robust, driving companies to invest significantly in innovative projects and R D. Yet, the dark cloud of rising oil prices threatens to dampen this enthusiasm. Nonetheless, this surge of positivity is the strongest we've witnessed in ten years.

articles.title: Eased FDI norms balance capital inflows and strategic safeguards: Experts
articles.summary: India's relaxed FDI norms for land-bordering countries aim to attract capital and technology while safeguarding interests. Experts highlight the pragmatic balance achieved, with a 60-day expedited approval for specified sectors and clarity on beneficial ownership, fostering supply-chain integration and boosting manufacturing.

articles.title: FDI easing not for Chinese firms; to benefit entities with minority Chinese holding
articles.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules. Overseas firms with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest automatically. Companies from China and nations sharing land borders with India will get faster approvals for investments in key manufacturing sectors. This aims to boost domestic production and attract foreign capital. The government is streamlining processes for greater certainty for investors.

articles.title: Housing sales in 50 cities dip 3% to 6.14 lakh units, up 16%  in value to Rs 8.4 lakh crore: CREDAI
articles.summary: Indian housing sales saw a slight dip in units last year but a significant rise in value. Demand for homes above Rs 1 crore drove this growth. Tier-2 cities are becoming key growth areas. Improved connectivity and job opportunities are boosting housing markets. Metro cities continue to lead, but smaller cities are gaining importance.

articles.title: Stay patient, volatility temporary, says Sebi Chairman as Iran-Israel war ruffles global markets
articles.summary: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey reassured investors that market volatility, fueled by geopolitical tensions like the Iran-Israel/US conflict, is temporary. He emphasized that the true test of financial markets lies in their ability to function smoothly, fairly, and efficiently despite uncertainty, drawing parallels to past disruptions like the COVID-19 pandemic and the Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.title: Rising concerns over India’s LPG supply: Causes, constraints & market implications
articles.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz are raising concerns over India’s LPG supply. With tanker movement slowing and imports falling, the government has prioritised household distribution and boosted domestic production while exploring alternative sources to cushion the impact of supply constraints.

articles.title: India revisits Press Note 3: Key clarifications to FDI framework for investments from land-bordering countries
articles.summary: India has clarified key aspects of the Press Note 3 framework governing investments from land-bordering countries, introducing a 10% beneficial ownership threshold and time-bound approvals for select sectors. The changes aim to reduce regulatory uncertainty while balancing national security concerns with the need to facilitate foreign investment.

The changes aim to reduce regulatory uncertainty while balancing national security concerns with the need to facilitate foreign investment.

articles.title: BSE, NSE organise mock trading session today: Check timing, purpose, other details
articles.summary: BSE and National Stock Exchange of India are conducting a mock trading session on Saturday across equity, commodity and currency derivatives segments. The exercise will test system readiness, disaster recovery infrastructure and trading applications, while helping brokers and market participants familiarise themselves with exchange processes under simulated market conditions.

articles.title: FIIs sell Indian equities worth Rs 52,704 crore in March, so far; Friday records its highest single-day outflow in 2026
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) sold Indian equities worth Rs 52,704 crore in the first half of March, with the biggest single-day outflow of 2026 recorded on Friday. The continued selling comes amid escalating geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran-Israel-US conflict and rising global uncertainty affecting investor sentiment.

articles.title: IPO Calendar: Coal India subsidiary among 3 IPOs scheduled for next week
articles.summary: India’s primary market will see three IPOs, including two mainboard issues and one SME offering in the upcoming week. GSP Crop Science and CMPDI, a Coal India subsidiary, are the highlights of the lineup, while Novus Loyalty launches on the SME platform. Additionally, three companies – Rajputana Stainless, Apsis Aerocom, and Raajmarg Infra – are slated to list, keeping market activity strong.

articles.title: Crude futures turn positive on continued Hormuz closure

The US has allowed countries to buy Russian oil to stabilize markets and plans to release oil reserves. Concerns about potential damage to oil infrastructure are growing, which could affect global supply and prices.

articles.title: Stock impact: 13 biggest losers in energy crisis amid raging Iran-Israel war
articles.summary: The Iran-Israel war has disrupted oil and gas supplies, hurting Indian oil marketing companies, auto manufacturers, fertilizer producers, and airlines. Food delivery and quick-service restaurant chains are less affected. The crisis highlights sector-specific vulnerabilities in India's stock market.
articles.raw_summary: Rising oil prices and gas supply disruptions amid the Iran–Israel war have hammered OMCs, RAC makers, fertiliser producers, and airlines, while food delivery and QSR chains show relative resilience, highlighting sector-specific vulnerabilities on Dalal Street.

With foreign capital fleeing the market, the Reserve Bank of India is taking measures to curb the Rupee's descent.

articles.title: AI cannot replace trained mind of lawyer, ethical responsibility of judge: Justice Vikram Nath
articles.summary: Justice Vikram Nath of the Supreme Court emphasized that AI cannot replace the trained mind of a lawyer or the ethical responsibility of a judge. He stated that while technology may assist in drafting notes, it should not be allowed to invent laws. He also warned against the misuse of AI leading to an extreme rejection of its use in the judicial system.
articles.raw_summary: Addressing a conference, Justice Nath further said technology may help draft a note, but it cannot be permitted to invent the law; at the same time, the misuse of AI cannot lead the judicial system to the opposite extreme of refusing to engage with it altogether.

articles.title: Haryana uses AI to speed up TB elimination: Senior official
articles.summary: Haryana is deploying three AI tools to accelerate tuberculosis elimination. These tools help detect TB cases early, monitor patients, and identify high-risk areas. The state aims to improve care accessibility and efficiency, with the goal of making Haryana TB-free.
articles.raw_summary: Haryana is using three Artificial Intelligence tools to combat tuberculosis. These AI solutions help detect TB cases early, monitor patients effectively, and identify high-risk areas. The state has already seen significant engagement with these technologies, flagging potential TB cases and identifying vulnerable communities. This technological push aims to make Haryana TB-free by improving care accessibility and efficiency.

articles.title: Group of Muslims breaking Ramzan fast attacked by mob in Pune; FIR filed
articles.summary: A mob of around 200 people attacked 14 Muslims who were breaking their Ramzan fast near Bopdeo Ghat in Pune. The police have registered an FIR and launched an investigation. The incident highlights concerns about communal violence and civil liberties in India.
articles.raw_summary: Fourteen Muslims were attacked by a mob of nearly 200 while breaking Ramzan fast near Bopdeo Ghat in Pune; police registered an FIR and launched a probe

articles.title: ‘No space for agitation’: Reactions pour in on Sonam Wangchuk release decision, Ladakh L-G calls for dialogue
articles.summary: Ladakh Lieutenant Governor Vinai Saxena described the release of activist Sonam Wangchuk as a 'positive step' that would foster peace and trust in the region. The decision follows Wangchuk's detention, and Saxena called for dialogue to address concerns. The move has implications for civil liberties and governance in Ladakh.
articles.raw_summary: Vinai Saxena described Sonam Wangchuk’s release as a “positive step” that would help foster “peace, stability and mutual trust in Ladakh”.

articles.title: IndiGo adds ‘fuel charge’ on flight ticket prices as oil spikes amid Iran war
articles.summary: IndiGo has introduced a smaller fuel charge on flight tickets due to the surge in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices. The airline says a larger adjustment was needed but opted for a smaller increase. This move comes amid rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, which could impact air travel costs and potentially lead to policy discussions on fuel pricing or aviation regulations.
articles.raw_summary: IndiGo says it has opted for a smaller fuel charge despite the surge in ATF prices requiring a “substantial adjustment” to base flight ticket prices.
